channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: CCP Announces Winners for 2026 EVE Frontier x SUI Foundation Hackathon
item.content:encoded:
Christina Gonzalez Updated: Apr 24, 2026 2:46 PM Posted: Apr 24, 2026 2:45 PM ET
Category: News 0

The 2026 EVE Frontier × Sui Foundation Hackathon now has its winners. CCP announced the winners and their creations, chosen from a field of more than 800 participants and 120 projects. The winners include a civilization management system, a bounty organization and execution (yes, we said it) system, and a way to make trading a social space.

This was the game’s fourth hackathon, and this time, the theme was “A Toolkit for Civilization”.

Top prize went to modder Reality Anchor’s CradleOS, a civilization management system with several features that include tools to let you coordinate resources, govern your territory, manage your defenses, and run logistics through on-chain infrastructure. This will let you take a more organized approach to creating and operating, and even managing a society. For CradleOS,  the prizes are $25,000 split between $15,000 in cash, and $10,000 in SUI tokens, along with a trip to FanFest 2026 next month. 

In second place was Blood Contract, which functions effectively as a bounty dashboard. you can set bounties on targets, define hunt conditions, and even automates payouts for successful completion. This gives the opportunity to turn this form of PVP into a more structured activity with instant gratification.

Third place went to Civilization Control, another control system that helps you manage infrastructure and defenses in a single interface, set rules, and more in one central tool. 

There were also several category winners: Utility, Creative, Live Frontier Integration, Technical Implementation, and Weirdest Idea.

Best Utility went to EasyAssemblies, a tool to let anyone configure Smart Assemblies.This one is beginner-friendly, and uses a visual interface to let you easily and quickly customize gates, defenses, storage, and other structures.

Frontier Flow was top for Technical Implementation. It’s an open-source tool to build Smart Assemblies through a simplified interface that lets you build and deploy automation, all generating SUI Move code without the need to directly code anything.

Best Creative went to Bazaar, which lets you turn trading from a menu into a walkable, shared social space. Instead of just browsing through a menu, you can browse through shops and interact with them inside a Bazaar. 

Live Frontier Integration went to Frontier Factional Warfare. This lets you create live conflict zones with capturable objectives. Those participating can join factions and fight over control of these objectives. The rules are meant to be enforced by in-world structures.

Finally, the Best Weird Idea pick went to Shadow Broker Protocol, which turns data and intelligence into  a commodity and a type of resource. This lets you buy, sell, and weaponized data and Intel creating another layer of power and making spycraft profitable. 

You can catch demos and more details of all of the winners over in the official announcement.

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ccp-announces-winners-for-2026-eve-frontier-x-sui-foundation-hackathon-2000137892
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Grinding Gear Games Sets Reveal Details for Path of Exile 2's Huge Return of the Ancients Update
item.content:encoded:
Grinding Gear Games is getting close to revealing all about Path of Exile 2’s next big content update, Return of the Ancients.

The next Grinding Gear Games live is happening on May 7th, and the team includes an announcement teaser to tide us over for now. The previously teased as huge 0.5.0 update launch  is coming on May 29th at 1:00 p.m. PDT on PC and console.  before then though, we'll find out everything on May 7th when the stream happens at 1:00 p.m. Pacific / 4:00 p.m. Eastern on Twitch.

As usual, the GGG team will detail everything coming in the major update, which they’re teasing with “ancient secrets rise”. The teaser hints at all that nicely. There is a giant fortress, giant enough to span continents, suddenly in Wraeclast. It’s full of secrets. And challenges. And more that we’ll find out about that’s to be revealed behind its walls. 

Expect to learn about the narrative content on the way, endgame revamps, likely item details, and more. There will be Twitch Drops for tuning in, which will be revealed closer to the stream date. 

After the big reveals, once again, we’ll get a community Q&A with ZiggyD, Jonathan, and Mark answering what they can on everything they’ve just revealed and more beyond.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/grinding-gear-games-sets-reveal-details-for-path-of-exile-2s-huge-return-of-the-ancients-update-2000137893
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
